elements.sent-2411-001: Heavy-flavour hadrons, containing at least one charm or beauty quark, are excellent probes of the deconfined medium created in ultra-relativistic heavy-ion collisions, known as quark--gluon plasma.
elements.sent-2411-002: Results in smaller collision systems, such as proton--proton and p--Pb collisions, besides representing an important baseline for interpreting heavy-ion measurements, are crucial to test perturbative QCD calculations and hadronisation mechanisms in the absence of hot medium effects, as well as to search for commonalities with heavy-ion systems.
elements.sent-2411-003: Recently, measurements in proton--proton and p--Pb collisions have revealed unforeseen features with respect to the expectations based on previous results from ${\rm e ^{+ {\rm e ^{-}$ and ep collisions, showing that fragmentation fractions of heavy quarks are not universal.}}}
elements.sent-2411-004: In this contribution, an overview of the most recent ALICE heavy-flavour measurements, along with the comparison to available calculations, will be discussed.
elements.sent-2411-005: Quarkonia, bound states of a $\rm c \overline{\rm c}$ or a $\rm b \overline{\rm b}$ pair, and open heavy-flavour (HF) hadrons, which have non-zero charm or beauty quantum numbers, are effective probes of several aspects of quantum chromodynamics (QCD).
elements.sent-2411-006: Comprehensive measurements of quarkonia and open HF hadrons in Pb--Pb collisions allow for testing the mechanisms of heavy-quark transport, energy loss, and coalescence effects during hadronisation in the presence of the quark--gluon plasma (QGP).
elements.sent-2411-007: In order to correctly interpret results in Pb--Pb collisions, measurements in proton--proton (pp) and p--Pb collisions are fundamental baselines.
elements.sent-2411-008: In particular, pp collisions provide details on the vacuum production, and studies in p--Pb collisions are useful to examine the so-called cold nuclear matter effects.
elements.sent-2411-009: Besides serving as reference, HF measurements in hadronic collisions provide a stringent test of QCD since the large quark masses imply high momentum transfer processes that make perturbative QCD (pQCD) calculations applicable to a large extent.
elements.sent-2411-010: According to the factorisation approach~\cite{Factorisation , the production cross section of HF hadrons in hadronic collisions can be described as the convolution of three terms: the parton distribution functions (PDFs) of the colliding nucleons; the partonic cross-section responsible for the production of the heavy-quark pair, which can be computed using pQCD calculations; and the non-perturbative evolution into the bound states.}
elements.sent-2411-011: Concerning the latter contribution, in the case of quarkonia different semi-phenomenological approaches, such as those implemented in the Color Evaporation Model (CEM) and Non-Relativistic QCD (NRQCD), are employed (see Ref.~\cite{Andronic for a comprehensive review).}
elements.sent-2411-012: For open HF hadrons, fragmentation functions, which describe the fraction of the heavy-quark momentum carried by the HF hadron, are considered.
elements.sent-2411-013: In the factorisation approach, the hadronisation process is taken to be independent of the collision system, and this assumption implies identical fragmentation fractions and fragmentation functions for ${\rm e ^{+ {\rm e ^{-}$, ep and pp collisions.}}}
elements.sent-2411-014: However, in the last decade a large wealth of results related to HF baryon production have disproved the assumed universality of the fragmentation fractions.
elements.sent-2411-015: In particular, an enhancement was observed for the first time by the ALICE Collaboration at midrapidity in pp collisions for the prompt $\Lambda_{\rm c ^{+}$/${\rm D ^{0}$ production yield ratio compared to similar results in leptonic collisions~\cite{LambdaCEnh , and this is even more pronounced for charm-strange baryon states production relative to ${\rm D ^{0}$ mesons~\cite{XicAndOmegaEnh .}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-016: In the beauty sector, the measurement of the $\Lambda_{\rm b ^{0}$ baryon production relative to that of beauty mesons at forward rapidity from the LHCb Collaboration~\cite{LHCb:2019fns shows an enhancement of the corresponding fragmentation fraction in pp collisions similar to that observed for charm quarks at midrapidity.}}
elements.sent-2411-017: As a consequence, state-of-the-art calculations employing a model of quark fragmentation constrained by leptonic measurements, such as those considered in PYTHIA 8 with the default tune (Monash)~\cite{pythia8monash , are challenged.}
elements.sent-2411-018: Several models attempt to reproduce the observed baryon enhancement assuming either modified hadronisation in a parton-rich environment with respect to in-vacuum fragmentation via different mechanisms, such as colour reconnection beyond the leading-colour approximation (CR-BLC)~\cite{PythiaBLC in PYTHIA 8 and coalescence~\cite{Catania,Powlang , or enhanced baryon production originating from the decay of unobserved charm-baryon resonant states~\cite{He .}}}
elements.sent-2411-019: It went through a major upgrade during Long Shutdown 2 (2019-2021)~\cite{ALICEupgrade of the LHC and restarted data taking at the beginning of LHC Run 3 in 2022.}
elements.sent-2411-020: The main upgraded detector subsystems in the central barrel are the Time Projection Chamber (TPC) and the Inner Tracking System (ITS).
elements.sent-2411-021: In particular, the upgraded readout system of the TPC allows it to cope with a continuous readout mode in Run 3, while the upgraded ITS provides an approximately 3 (6) times improvement in pointing resolution in the transverse (longitudinal) direction.
elements.sent-2411-022: In addition, the Muon Forward Tracker was installed at forward rapidity, enabling the reconstruction of secondary vertices from charm- and beauty-hadron decays in the acceptance of the muon spectrometer.
elements.sent-2411-023: Figure~\ref{JpsiCrossSec shows the new preliminary measurement of the inclusive J/$\psi$ cross section reconstructed at midrapidity in pp collisions at $\sqrt{s}$ = 13.6 TeV.}
elements.sent-2411-024: In the left panel, the results are compared with similar midrapidity Run 2 measurements from ALICE and ATLAS (see references on the left panel), the latter available for $p_{\rm T > 7$~GeV/$c$.}
elements.sent-2411-025: The comparison indicates a good agreement between Run 2 and Run 3 results, as well as a significantly improved granularity with respect to previous ALICE measurements.
elements.sent-2411-026: The cross section is described within uncertainties by NRQCD-based models~\cite{Buth,Ma,Ma1 and an improved version of the CEM~\cite{icem , both coupled to FONLL~\cite{fonll to account for the contribution of J/$\psi$ originating from beauty hadron decays, as shown in the right panel of Fig.~\ref{JpsiCrossSec .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-027: The large uncertainties of the model calculations do not allow for discriminating among different models, however these measurements represent an important input for tuning such models, especially in the low-$p_{\rm T}$ region.
elements.figure-2411-001: \includegraphics[width=6.4cm,clip]{figs/jpsi_crosssection_pt_compare.pdf} \includegraphics[width=6.4cm,clip]{figs/jpsi_crosssection_pt_run3_allmodel_wfonll.pdf} \caption{Inclusive J/$\psi$ cross section measured at midrapidity in pp collisions at $\sqrt{s}$ = 13.6 TeV compared to similar measurements from ALICE and ATLAS at $\sqrt{s}$ = 13 TeV in the left panel (see references on the plot) and with several charmonium production models~\cite{Buth,Ma,Ma1,icem} coupled to FONLL~\cite{fonll} in the right panel.} \label{JpsiCrossSec}
elements.sent-2411-028: The prompt ${\rm D _{\rm s ^{+}$/$\rm D^{+}$ production yield ratio at midrapidity in pp collisions at $\sqrt{s}$ = 13.6 TeV is depicted in Fig.~\ref{DmesonRatios .}}}
elements.sent-2411-029: The comparison to previous Run 2 results in the left panel clearly shows that preliminary Run 3 measurements are compatible with published results at lower centre-of-mass energies.
elements.figure-2411-002: \includegraphics[width=5.2cm,clip]{figs/dsoverdpluscomparisonalice_0.pdf} \includegraphics[width=5.2cm,clip]{figs/dsoverdpluscomparisonmodels_0.pdf} \caption{Prompt ${\rm D}_{\rm s}^{+}$/${\rm D}^{+}$ production yield ratio measured at midrapidity in pp collisions at $\sqrt{s}$ = 13.6 TeV compared to similar measurements at lower centre-of-mass energies from Run 2 in the left panel (see references on the plot) and with model calculations~\cite{pythia8monash,PythiaBLC,Catania,Powlang} in the right panel. } \label{DmesonRatios}
elements.sent-2411-030: Furthermore, they have significantly increased granularity and are extended down to a lower $p_{\rm T}$ (0.5 GeV/$c$) compared to previous measurements.
elements.sent-2411-031: In the right panel of Fig.~\ref{DmesonRatios , the prompt ${\rm D _{\rm s ^{+}$/$\rm D^{+}$ production yield ratio is compared to model calculations.}}}
elements.sent-2411-032: PYTHIA 8 calculations with Monash~\cite{pythia8monash and CR-BLC~\cite{PythiaBLC tunes, the latter introduced to improve the description for the baryons, underpredict the data, while POWLANG~\cite{Powlang , which assumes an expanding fireball in pp collisions and hadronisation occurring via coalescence, overshoots the measured ratio.}}}
elements.sent-2411-033: The Catania~\cite{Catania model, which considers both coalescence and fragmentation for the charm quark hadronisation, provides a description closer to the data.}
elements.figure-2411-003: \includegraphics[width=5.6cm,clip]{figs/results_scratio_6.pdf} \includegraphics[width=5.6cm,clip]{figs/results_scratio_4.pdf} \caption{Relative production of the $\Sigma_{\rm c}^{0,++}(2520)$ baryon to the ground state $\Sigma_{\rm c}^{0,++}(2455)$ measured at midrapidity in pp collisions at $\sqrt{s}$ = 13.6 TeV. In the left panel, it is compared with model calculations~\cite{pythia8monash,PythiaBLC,He,SHM} and to lower energy results from Belle Collaboration (see reference on the plot). In the right panel, the ratio is compared with PYTHIA 8 CR-BLC calculations~\cite{PythiaBLC} after tuning one parameter of the model (see text for details). } \label{Sigmac}
elements.sent-2411-034: In the left panel of Fig.~\ref{Sigmac , the ratio is compared to several model calculations~\cite{pythia8monash,PythiaBLC,He,SHM and to similar measurements in ${\rm e ^{+ {\rm e ^{-}$ collisions at $\sqrt{s}$ = 10.52 GeV from the Belle Collaboration (see reference on the figure).}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-035: No evidence of an enhancement with respect to ${\rm e ^{+ {\rm e ^{-}$ collisions is observed considering the current uncertainties.}}}
elements.sent-2411-036: Models struggle to reproduce the data; in particular, PYTHIA 8 with the Monash tune overestimates the data, while the CR-BLC tune, which describes the prompt $\Lambda_{\rm c ^{+}$/${\rm D ^{0}$ production yield ratio fairly well, significantly underestimates the $\Sigma_{\rm c ^{0,++ (2520)$/$\Sigma_{\rm c ^{0,++ (2455)$ yield ratio.}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-037: The statistical hadronisation model (SHMc)~\cite{SHM , which assumes that the abundances of all particles, including open and hidden charm hadrons, are determined by thermal weights at the chemical freeze-out, provides the $p_{\rm T}$-integrated $\Sigma_{\rm c ^{0,++ (2520)$/$\Sigma_{\rm c ^{0,++ (2455)$ ratio which is consistent with the $p_{\rm T}$-differential measurement from ALICE.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-038: The statistical hadronisation model coupled with the Relativistic Quark Model (SHM+RQM)~\cite{He , for which an augmented baryon production is obtained from the significant feed-down from excited charm baryon states beyond those listed in the PDG, predicts lower values compared to the measurement.}
elements.sent-2411-039: An interesting example of how new measurements can be used to provide constraints for model tuning is shown in the right panel of Fig.~\ref{Sigmac .}
elements.sent-2411-040: In particular, the $\Sigma_{\rm c ^{0,++ (2520)$/$\Sigma_{\rm c ^{0,++ (2455)$ production yield ratio can be reproduced by PYTHIA 8 calculations with the CR-BLC tune after the parameter that suppresses spin-one with respect to spin-zero charm-light diquark systems is tuned on the measured $\Lambda_{\rm c ^{+ (\leftarrow \Sigma_{\rm c ^{0,++ )/ \Lambda_{\rm c ^{+}$ production yield ratio (see details in Ref.~\cite{Altmann ).}}}}}}}}}}
elements.section-2411-003: Highlights from p--Pb and Pb--Pb collisions
elements.sent-2411-041: In the left panel of Fig.~\ref{Xic , the prompt $\Xi_{\rm c ^{0}$ $p_{\rm T}$-differential cross section in p--Pb collisions at $\sqrt{s_{\rm NN }$ = 5.02 TeV~\cite{arxivPPb is compared with POWHEG+PYTHIA 6 simulations coupled to the EPPS16 nPDFs (see references in~\cite{arxivPPb ).}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-042: These predictions, which incorporate fragmentation fractions from ${\rm e ^{+ {\rm e ^{-}$ collisions, significantly underestimate the prompt $\Xi_{\rm c ^{0}$ production.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-043: The agreement improves with the predictions from Quark Combination Model (QCM), which assumes the coalescence of charm- and light-flavour quarks close in the momentum space, without considering the coordinate space.
elements.sent-2411-044: However, also in this case the prompt $\Xi_{\rm c ^{0}$ cross section remains underestimated by a factor of 2.}
elements.sent-2411-045: The measurement of the $\Xi_{\rm c ^{0}$ production combined with the others already available for charm mesons and baryons in p--Pb collisions at $\sqrt{s_{\rm NN }$ = 5.02 TeV, allowed for measuring fragmentation fractions of charm hadrons in this collision system.}}
elements.sent-2411-046: They are shown in the right panel of Fig.~\ref{Xic where they are compared with results from pp collisions at $\sqrt{s}$ = 5.02 TeV as well as results from ${\rm e ^{+ {\rm e ^{-}$ and ep collisions at lower $\sqrt{s}$ (see~\cite{arxivPPb2 and references therein).}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-047: The fragmentation fractions in p--Pb collisions are consistent with those in pp collisions, showing no dependence on the system size at the LHC.
elements.sent-2411-048: Furthermore, they are significantly enhanced (diminished) compared to those from leptonic collisions in the case of baryons (mesons), confirming the hypothesis of differing hadronisation in a parton-rich environment as already argued from previous pp measurements.
elements.figure-2411-004: \includegraphics[width=4.7cm,clip]{figs/CompareCrossSecVsModels.pdf} \includegraphics[width=5.9cm,clip]{figs/FF5tev_withpPbAndJpsi.pdf} \caption{Left panel: prompt $\Xi_{c}^{0}$ $p_{\rm T}$-differential cross section in p--Pb collisions at $\sqrt{s_{\rm NN}}$ = 5.02 TeV compared with POWHEG+PYTHIA 6 simulations coupled to the EPPS16 nPDF parametrisation and to QCM (see~\cite{arxivPPb} and references therein). Right panel: fragmentation fractions for charm hadrons in p--Pb collisions at $\sqrt{s_{\rm NN}}$ = 5.02 TeV, compared to results from pp collisions at $\sqrt{s}$ = 5.02 TeV and from ${\rm e}^{+}{\rm e}^{-}$ and ep collisions at lower energies (see~\cite{arxivPPb2} and references therein). } \label{Xic}
elements.sent-2411-049: In the left panel of Fig.~\ref{JpsiRaa , the $p_{\rm T}$-differential prompt J/$\psi$ nuclear modification factor $R_{\rm AA}$, measured in the centrality class 0--10\%, is compared to similar measurements from the CMS and ATLAS Collaborations, as well as with several model calculations (see references in ~\cite{PbPbJpsi ).}}
elements.sent-2411-050: Results from ALICE nicely extend high-$p_{\rm T}$ measurements down to 1.5 GeV/$c$, and are in good agreement with CMS and ATLAS measurements in the overlapping $p_{\rm T}$ intervals.
elements.sent-2411-051: The comparison with calculations suggests that models which include re-combination either at the chemical freeze-out (SHMc) or during the QGP phase (Boltzmann Transport, BT) are able to describe the rising trend of the $R_{\rm AA}$ down to low $p_{\rm T}$.
elements.sent-2411-052: In the right panel of Fig.~\ref{JpsiRaa , the $p_{\rm T}$-integrated ($1.5 < p_{\rm T < 10$ GeV/$c$) prompt J/$\psi$ $R_{\rm AA}$ is shown as a function of centrality (expressed by the average number of participants, $\langle N_{\rm part \rangle$), where it is compared to the BT model.}}}
elements.sent-2411-053: Experimental data show a rising trend with increasing centrality, as expected from the re-generation scenario, and are well described within uncertainties by the BT model above $\langle N_{\rm part \rangle \approx 50$.}
elements.figure-2411-005: \includegraphics[width=6.0cm,clip]{figs/promptraa010withexperimentsandmodels.pdf} \includegraphics[width=6.0cm,clip]{figs/PromptRaaCentWithModels.pdf} \caption{Left panel: $p_{\rm T}$-differential prompt J/$\psi$ $R_{\rm AA}$ measured at midrapidity in Pb--Pb collisions (0--10\% centrality class) at $\sqrt{s_{\rm NN}}$ = 5.02 TeV compared to similar measurements from other experiments and with theoretical models (see~\cite{PbPbJpsi} and references therein). Right panel: prompt J/$\psi$ $R_{\rm AA}$ for $1.5 < p_{\rm T} < 10$ GeV/$c$ as a function of $\langle N_{\rm part} \rangle$ compared with BT calculations (see~\cite{PbPbJpsi} and references therein).} \label{JpsiRaa}
elements.sent-2411-054: The ALICE Collaboration produced a large collection of new HF physics results based on Run 2 and fresh Run 3 data, the latter collected using the upgraded ALICE detector.
elements.sent-2411-055: In these proceedings, a few selected highlights from different collision systems are presented.
elements.sent-2411-056: Run 3 data taking is efficiently progressing, and will continue until the end of 2025.
elements.sent-2411-057: Several upgrade projects are expected to be installed during Long Shutdown 3 (2026-2028).
elements.sent-2411-058: Among others, the ITS3~\cite{its3 project foresees to replace the innermost 3 layers of the current ITS with truly cylindrical silicon layers, providing an improvement of the pointing resolution, crucial for the reconstruction of HF hadrons, by a factor 2 compared to the current ITS.}
elements.sent-2411-059: These improvements, as well as the increase in terms of amount of collected data, which by the end of Run 4 is expected to be about 100 (1000) times larger for Pb--Pb (pp and p--Pb) compared to the total recorded during the Runs 1 and 2, will allow for high-precision HF measurements, including direct beauty reconstruction and the production of multi-charm baryons.
elements.sent-2411-060: Heavy-flavour hadrons, i.e. bound states containing at least one charm or beauty quark, are excellent probes of the hot and dense medium created in ultra-relativistic heavy-ion collisions, known as quark-gluon plasma.
elements.sent-2411-061: Results in small systems, such as proton-proton and p--Pb collisions, besides representing an important baseline for interpreting heavy-ion measurements, are crucial to test perturbative-QCD calculations and hadronisation mechanisms in absence of hot medium effects, as well as for studying commonalities with heavy-ion system.
elements.sent-2411-062: Recently, measurements performed in proton-proton and p--Pb collisions have revealed unforeseen features with respect to the expectations based on previous measurements from ${\rm e ^{+ {\rm e ^{-}$ and and ep collisions, showing that hadronisation fractions of heavy quarks are not universal.}}}
elements.sent-2411-063: In this contribution, an overview of the most recent ALICE measurements of hidden and open heavy-flavour hadrons in small and large collision systems will be presented.
elements.sent-2411-064: Results will be discussed along with the comparison to the corresponding available theoretical calculations.
elements.sent-2411-065: Quarkonia, bound states of a $\rm c \bar{\rm c}$ or a $\rm b \bar{\rm b}$ pair, together with open heavy-flavour (HF) hadrons, the latter containing a charm or beauty quark and a light quark, are effective probes of several aspects of quantum cromodynamics (QCD).
elements.sent-2411-066: In heavy-ion collisions, HF quarks are produced in the initial hard partonic scatterings, thus they experience the full collision history.
elements.sent-2411-067: Comprehensive measurements of quarkonia and open HF hadrons in Pb--Pb collisions, allow for testing the mechanisms of heavy-quark transport, energy loss, and coalescence effects during the hadronisation in the presence of the hot medium.
elements.sent-2411-068: In order to interpret correctly results in Pb--Pb collisions, measurements in small systems, namely proton-propton (pp) and p--Pb collisions are fundamental baseline.
elements.sent-2411-069: In particular, pp collisions provide details on the vacuum production while studies in p--Pb collisions serve as an additional baseline to examine so-called cold nuclear matter effects.
elements.sent-2411-070: Besides serving as reference, HF measurements in hadronic collisions provide a stringent test of QCD since the large quark masses provide hard scales that make perturbative calculations applicable to a large extent.
elements.sent-2411-071: According to a factorisation approach~\cite{Factorisation , the production cross section of HF hadrons in ultrarelativistic hadronic collisions can be described as the convolution of three contributions: the parton distribution functions (PDFs) of the colliding nucleons, the cross-section of the hard parton scattering responsible for the production of HF quark pair, which can be calculated using perturbative-QCD (pQCD) calculations, and the non-perturbative evolution into the bound states.}
elements.sent-2411-072: In case of quarkonia, different semi-phenomenological approaches such as those in the Color Evaporation Model (CEM) and Non-Relativistic QCD (NRQCD) are employed (see Ref.~\cite{Andronic for a comphrensive review), while for open HF hadrons, fragmentation functions which describe the fraction of the heavy-quark momentum carried by the heavy-flavour hadron are considered.}
elements.sent-2411-073: Typically, in the factorisation approach, the hadronisation process is taken to be independent of the collision system, and this assumption would imply identical fragmentation fractions between ${\rm e ^{+ {\rm e ^{-}$, ep and pp collisions.}}}
elements.sent-2411-074: However, in the last decade a large wealth of results related to HF baryon production have disproved the fragmentation universality assumption.
elements.sent-2411-075: In particular, an enhancement was observed for the first time by ALICE at midrapidity in pp collisions at the LHC for the production yield ratio of $\Lambda_{\rm c ^{+}$ to ${\rm D ^{0}$ compared to similar results in leptonic collisions~\cite{LambdaCEnh , and this is even more pronounced for charm-strange baryon states~\cite{XicAndOmegaEnh .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-076: In the beauty sector, the measurements of $\Lambda_{\rm b ^{0}$-baryon production relative to that of beauty mesons at forward rapidity from LHCb~\cite{LHCb:2019fns show a modification of the fragmentation fractions among collision systems similar to that observed for charm quarks at midrapidity.}}
elements.sent-2411-077: As a consequence, state-of-art pQCD calculations employing a model of quark fragmentation constrained by leptonic measurements, such as Pythia8 with default tune (Monash)~\cite{pythia8monash , are challenged.}
elements.sent-2411-078: Several models attempt to reproduce the observed baryon enhancement assuming a modified hadronisation in a parton rich enviroenment w.r.t.
elements.sent-2411-079: It went through a major upgrade during the Long Shutdown 2 (2019-2021)~\cite{ALICEupgrade and restarted data taking since the beginning of the LHC Run 3 in 2022.}
elements.sent-2411-080: In particular, the upgraded readout system of the TPC allows to cope with the continuos redout mode in Run 3, while the upgraded ITS provides an approximately 3 to 6 times improvement in pointing resolution.
elements.sent-2411-081: In addition, the Muon Forward Tracker (MFT) was installed at forward rapidity, enabling the reconstruction of secondary vertices from beauty-hadron decays in the acceptance of the ALICE muon spectrometer.
elements.sent-2411-082: Figure~\ref{JpsiCrossSec shows new preliminary measurement of the inclusive J/$\psi$ cross section reconstructed at midrapidity via the dielectron decay channel in pp collisions at $\sqrt{s}$ = 13.6 TeV.}
elements.sent-2411-083: In the left panel, results are compared with similar midrapidity Run 2 measurements from ALICE and ATLAS (see references on the left panel), the latter available for transverse momentum ($p_{\rm T}$) larger than 7 GeV/$c$.
elements.sent-2411-084: The comparison denotes a good agreement of new results with Run 2 data, as well as a significantly improved granularity w.r.t.. previous ALICE measurements.
elements.sent-2411-085: The cross section is described within uncertainties by NRQCD based models~\cite{Buth,Ma,Ma1 and an improved version of the CEM~\cite{icem , both coupled to FONLL~\cite{fonll to account for the contribution of J/$\psi$ originating from beauty hadron decays, as shown in the right panel of Fig.~\ref{JpsiCrossSec .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-086: The large uncertainties of the model calculations do not allow to discriminate among different models, however these measurements represent an input for tuning such models, especially in the low $p_{\rm T}$ region.

elements.sent-2411-105: They are shown in the right panel of Fig.~\ref{Sigmac where they are compared with results from pp collisions at $\sqrt{s}$ = 5.02 TeV and from ${\rm e ^{+ {\rm e ^{-}$ and ep collisions at lower $\sqrt{s}$ (see~\cite{arxivPPb2 and references therein).}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-106: The hadronisation fractions in p--Pb are consistent with those in pp collisions, showing no dependence on the system size at the LHC.
elements.sent-2411-107: Furthermore, they are significantly enhanced compared to those from leptonic collisions, confirming the hypothesis of different hadronisation in a parton-rich environment as already observed in previous pp measurements.
elements.figure-2411-009: \includegraphics[width=6.4cm,clip]{figs/CompareCrossSecVsModels.pdf} \includegraphics[width=6.4cm,clip]{figs/FF5tev_withpPbAndJpsi.pdf} \caption{Left panel: prompt $\Xi_{c}^{0}$ $p_{\rm T}$-differential cross section in p--Pb collisions at $\sqrt{s}_{\rm NN}$ = 5.02 TeV compared to POWHEG+PYTHIA 6 simulations couple with the EPPS16 nPDF parameterisation and to QCM (see~\cite{arxivPPb} and references therein). Right panel: fragmentation fractions for charm hadrons in p--Pb collisions at $\sqrt{s}_{\rm NN}$ = 5.02 TeV, compared with results from pp collisions at $\sqrt{s}$ = 5.02 TeV and from ${\rm e}^{+}{\rm e}^{-}$ and ep collisions at lower energies (see~\cite{arxivPPb2} and references therein). } \label{Sigmac}
elements.sent-2411-108: In the left panel of Fig.~\ref{JpsiRaa , the $p_{\rm T}$-differential prompt J/$\psi$ nuclear modification factor $R_{\rm AA}$ in centrality 0--10\% is compared to similar measurements from CMS and ATLAS collaborations, as well as to several model calculations (see references in ~\cite{PbPbJpsi ).}}
elements.sent-2411-109: The comparison with calculations suggests that models which include re-combination either at the chemical freezeout (SHMc) or during the QGP phase (Boltzmann Transport, BT) are able to describe the raising trend of the $R_{\rm AA}$ down to low $p_{\rm T}$.
elements.sent-2411-110: The $p_{\rm T}$-integrated ($1.5 < p_{\rm T < 10$ GeV/$c$) prompt J/$\psi$ $R_{\rm AA}$ is shown as a function of centrality, where it is compared to BT model.}
elements.sent-2411-111: The latter shows a rising trend with increasing centrality (expressed in terms of average number of participants, $\langle N_{\rm part \rangle$) as observed in the data, and it is in good agreement with experimental results in 0–10\% and 10–30\% centrality classes.}
elements.figure-2411-010: \includegraphics[width=6.4cm,clip]{figs/promptraa010withexperimentsandmodels.pdf} \includegraphics[width=6.4cm,clip]{figs/PromptRaaCentWithModels.pdf} \caption{Left panel: $p_{\rm T}$-differential prompt J/$\psi$ $R_{\rm AA}$ measured at midrapidity in Pb--Pb collisions (0--10\% centrality) at $\sqrt{s}_{\rm NN}$ = 5.02 TeV compared with similar measurements from other experiments and to theoretical model calculations (see~\cite{PbPbJpsi} and references therein). Right panel: prompt J/$\psi$ $R_{\rm AA}$ for $1.5 < p_{\rm T} < 10$ GeV/$c$ as a function of centrality compared to theory calculations~\cite{PbPbJpsi}.} \label{JpsiRaa}
elements.sent-2411-112: The ALICE collaboration produced an impressive collection of new HF physics results based on Run 2 and fresh Run 3 data, the latter collected using the upgraded ALICE detector.
elements.sent-2411-113: In these proceedings a few selected highlights from different collision systems are presented.
elements.sent-2411-114: Efficient Run 3 data taking is ongoing, and it is foreseen until the end of 2025.
elements.sent-2411-115: Several upgrade projects are expected to be installed during the LHC Long Shutdown 3 (2025-2028), in particular the ITS3~\cite{its3 one foresees to replace the innermost 3 layers of the current ITS with truly cylindrical silicon layers, providing an improvement of the pointing resolution by a factor 2 compared to the current ITS.}
elements.sent-2411-116: These improvements, as well as the increase in terms of statistics, which by the end of Run 4 is expected to be about 1000 (100) times larger compared to the one recorded during the Run 1 and 2, will allow for high precision HF measurements.
